title: NSA releases joint advisory on state-sponsored cyber threats targeting critical infrastructure
summary: The National Security Agency published a joint cybersecurity advisory with Five Eyes partners warning of increased state-sponsored cyber operations targeting energy and telecommunications infrastructure.
contents:
# NSA Publishes Joint Five Eyes Cybersecurity Advisory

## Advisory Details
The National Security Agency released a joint cybersecurity advisory on May 1, 2026, in coordination with Five Eyes intelligence partners (GCHQ, CSE, ASD, GCSB), warning of increased state-sponsored cyber operations targeting critical infrastructure.

## Threat Assessment
- Identified threat actors target energy and telecommunications sectors
- Operations attributed to nation-state actors with sophisticated capabilities
- Pre-positioning activity detected in operational technology (OT) networks
- Advisory provides technical indicators and mitigation guidance

## Partner Coordination
The advisory represents coordinated intelligence sharing among:
- NSA (United States)
- GCHQ (United Kingdom)
- CSE (Canada)
- ASD (Australia)
- GCSB (New Zealand)

## Recommended Actions
Critical infrastructure operators should review network architectures, implement segmentation between IT and OT systems, and monitor for the specific indicators of compromise detailed in the advisory.

## Tags
nsa, cybersecurity, five-eyes, critical-infrastructure, state-sponsored, threat-advisory
